Resolves #15041

Registers `@neo-gpt-emmy` as the second GPT-family resident through the canonical roster-onboarding ceremony while keeping every fact that requires first boot explicitly pending. The committed root has its own mailbox, memory provenance, `CODEX_HOME` isolation gate, sibling/review semantics, and no inherited Euclid wake route or predicted engine facts.

Related: #13015

Evidence: L2 (generator write/idempotency, production-data migration probe, and 40 focused unit tests) → L2 required (all close-target ACs are committed-source and deterministic-contract checks). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket

- The first GPT-sibling run exposed `Gpt` title-casing in the generator; the source now preserves the `GPT` acronym and pins the rendered entry.
- Fan-out review exposed a migration interaction beyond the original four generated surfaces: post-epoch residents previously failed the legacy migration unless someone fabricated a model designation. The root now carries `eraSeedPolicy: 'post-epoch-no-backfill'`; migration defers it without failure so the first era remains observation-owned.
- The GitHub profile was populated during implementation. ModelStats records the account as operational evidence while explicitly refusing to treat its operator-provisioned name/bio as bearer assent or live engine observation.
- The concrete roster pin is stronger than the generic generator pin: exact uniqueness, sibling relation, same-family review semantics, separate memory provenance, isolated Codex prerequisites, non-active status, and the complete observation-owned absence set.

## Test Evidence

- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/setup/generateRosterOnboarding.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/graph/identityRoots.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/graph/identityRootsMigration.spec.mjs` — 40 passed.
- Direct production-data migration probe — `valid: true`, seven migration-epoch residents migrated, `@neo-gpt-emmy` explicitly skipped as `post-epoch-resident-no-seed-era`, zero failures.
- Exact generator rerun for `@neo-gpt-emmy` — all four surfaces reported `EXISTS`; no writes.
- `node --check` passed for the changed runtime modules.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `npm run agent-preflight -- --no-fix ...` passed before commit.
